sections.paragraph:
δ´ numerical sign = 4 (τέσσαρες: Jos., C. Ap. 1, 158; Mel., P. 90, 673 [B]; cp. SibOr 5, 40) Hs 9, 10, 7; or 4th (τετάρτη) in the titles Hv 4; m 4; Hs 4 (uncertain, s. the app.). ἐν τῷ δ´ τῆς ἐξηγήσεως τῶν κυριακῶν λόγων Papias (3:1).

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Hv Hv = Visions

m m = Mandates

app. app. = appendix, apparatus (when ref. to marginal text-critical information, esp. in N.)

Papias Papias , early Christian writer, II a.d.—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 209
